title: Restore locale and time zone after scoped tests
prompt:
Repair the order-dependent Java test failure in this repository. The US report case passes when run by itself, but the seeded full suite runs a localized report case first and the later case inherits its process-wide default locale and time zone. Keep the report formatting behavior and the `ScopedDefaults.runWith` API intact, including invoking its callback exactly once and propagating callback failures. Localize the environment change to the callback and restore the exact time zone and all locale defaults that were active on entry (the general default plus the DISPLAY and FORMAT category defaults), whether the callback returns or throws.

Reproduce both sides deterministically with `bash test.sh direct` and `bash test.sh seeded 10010`. The submitted fix must pass both commands; `bash test.sh` is the combined verification entry point. When a JDK is installed the runner executes the direct and pinned-shuffle checks in that order and verifies restoration as well as the inherited-state symptom; an offline protected source-contract fallback checks the same control-flow contract when no JDK is available. Do not add external dependencies, network access, wall-clock dependencies, unseeded randomness, sleeps, subprocesses, or test-order assumptions. Do not modify `src/test/java/`, `tests/`, or `test.sh`.
